meta.name: MERFISH clustering (imaging-based counts)
meta.description: Cluster imaging-based FISH data (MERFISH/MERSCOPE) the way the scanpy tutorial does: counts-per-million normalize (target_sum=1e6) → log1p → PCA on few components (n_comps=15, suited to small gene panels) → neighbors → UMAP → Leiden. Produces obs['leiden'].
meta.provenance: Adapted from the MERFISH section of the scanpy 'Analysis and visualization of spatial transcriptomics data' tutorial.

readme: The scanpy tutorial's MERFISH branch. Imaging-based FISH panels measure few genes at high counts, so this normalizes to counts-per-million (`target_sum=1e6`, the modern equivalent of the tutorial's `normalize_per_cell(counts_per_cell_after=1e6)`) and runs PCA with only 15 components before the standard neighbors → UMAP → Leiden path. Clusters land in `obs['leiden']`. Contrast with 'Preprocess & cluster (raw counts)', which uses default median normalization and full-width PCA for larger panels.
